story: Flags waved as the United Nations General Assembly gathered for its very first meeting in 1946. On January 10, representatives from countries around the world came together in London. story: The ground shook as a massive chunk of chalk cliff tumbled into the sea. On January 10, 2001, part of the famous Beachy Head in East Sussex, England, suddenly collapsed. story: Splash! Our oceans are getting toastier than scientists expected. On January 10, 2019, researchers shared surprising news in the journal Science. title: Breaking Barriers in the Senate
story: Edward W. Brooke made history on January 10, 1967. The Republican from Massachusetts became the first African American popularly elected to the US Senate. Brooke's groundbreaking achievement opened doors for future generations of diverse leaders in American politics. His election marked a significant step forward in the ongoing struggle for equal representation.

title: A Sculptor's Artistic Journey Begins
story: Barbara Hepworth's creative spark ignited on January 10, 1903. Born in Wakefield, Yorkshire, she would grow up to become a famous English abstract artist and sculptor. title: A Scarecrow Dances to Life
story: Ray Bolger leaped into the world on January 10, 1904, in Dorchester, Massachusetts. This bouncy baby boy would grow up to become a beloved dancer, actor, and singer. Bolger's most famous role? The lovable, floppy Scarecrow in The Wizard of Oz! He also delighted TV audiences as Grandpa Renfrew on The Partridge Family.
activity_question: What was Ray Bolger's most famous role?
activity_choices: The Scarecrow in The Wizard of Oz
Dorothy in The Wizard of Oz
Grandpa Renfrew on The Partridge Family

story: Gazing at the stars took on new meaning for Robert Woodrow Wilson.
